concepts.title: Five-Year Plans
concepts.description: The Five-Year Plans were a series of nation-wide centralized exercises in planning, in which targets were set for different sectors of the economy, and the allocation of resources was made to achieve those targets. Understanding the significance and objectives of these plans is crucial for the exam.

concepts.title: Importance of Planning
concepts.description: As per the NCERT textbook, planning is essential for a country like India to achieve rapid economic growth and development. It helps in allocating resources efficiently, reducing poverty and inequality, and promoting social welfare.

concepts.title: Role of Planning Commission
concepts.description: The Planning Commission played a vital role in the formulation of Five-Year Plans. It was responsible for assessing the country's resources, identifying priorities, and making allocation of resources to different sectors. Expect questions related to its functions and significance.

concepts.title: Remembering the Five-Year Plans
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'IGIMS' to remember the first six Five-Year Plans: I (First Plan, 1951-56), G (Second Plan, 1956-61, focused on Heavy Industry), I (Third Plan, 1961-66), M (Fourth Plan, 1969-74, focused on Minimum Needs), S (Fifth Plan, 1974-78, focused on Self-Reliance).

concepts.title: Confusing Planning Commission with NITI Aayog
concepts.description: Students often confuse the Planning Commission with NITI Aayog. Remember that the Planning Commission was replaced by NITI Aayog in 2015. While the Planning Commission was responsible for formulating Five-Year Plans, NITI Aayog focuses on cooperative federalism and providing strategic and technical advice to the government.

concepts.title: Key Components of Planning
concepts.description: Remember the key components of planning, including: (i) Perspective Planning, (ii) Annual Plans, (iii) Five-Year Plans, (iv) Long-Term Planning, and (v) Rolling Plans. Understanding these components is essential to grasp the concept of planning in India.

concepts.title: Objectives of Planning
concepts.description: The primary objectives of planning in India include: (i) Rapid economic growth, (ii) Social justice, (iii) Self-reliance, and (iv) Reduction of poverty and inequality. These objectives are crucial to understanding the context of planning in India.

concepts.title: Decentralized Planning
concepts.description: As per the NCERT textbook, decentralized planning involves the participation of local communities and panchayats in the planning process. This approach helps in making planning more inclusive and responsive to local needs.
